message:
fix(lua): handle nil assignment as deletion in LuaTable array and hash paths

Prevent nil values from being appended to the array part when
assigning to the next sequential index (idx == len+1), which would
grow the array with a tombstone entry that breaks ipairs/unpack
semantics. In the hash path, treat nil assignment as key deletion:
remove from string_hash for string keys, or from other_keys/other_hash
for non-string keys, matching Lua's standard table[key]=nil erasure
behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
